ddls:
CREATE TABLE "user_visits" (
  "visit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user visit */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who visited the page */,
  "url_id" INTEGER /* ID of the URL visited */,
  "visit_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the visit (in microseconds since epoch) */,
  "from_visit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the visit from which this visit originated */,
  "transition_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the transition type (e.g., typed URL, link click) */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment the visit belongs to */,
  "visit_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the visit in microseconds */,
  "incremented_omnibox_typed_score" INTEGER /* Increment in the omnibox typed score */,
  "opener_visit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the opener visit (if applicable) */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "referrer_url" TEXT /* Referrer URL that led to this visit */,
  "geolocation" TEXT /* Geolocation data (latitude, longitude, city, country) */,
  "is_first_visit" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if this is the user's first visit to the site */,
  "is_bot" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the visit is from a bot */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session to which this visit belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_visits_from_visit_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_visit_id") REFERENCES visit_sources ("from_visit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_visits_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_visits_url_id FOREIGN KEY ("url_id") REFERENCES urls ("url_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_visits_transition_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("transition_type_id") REFERENCES transition_types ("transition_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_visits_opener_visit_id FOREIGN KEY ("opener_visit_id") REFERENCES visit_opener ("opener_visit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_visits_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, data scientist, admin) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date and time of user registration */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive, banned) */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the user */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language preference of the user */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the user */,
  "last_ip" TEXT /* Last known IP address of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "urls" (
  "url_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each URL */,
  "url" TEXT /* Full URL string */,
  "domain" TEXT /* Domain of the URL */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the URL was first recorded */,
  "last_visited" TEXT /* Date and time of the last visit to this URL */,
  "visit_count" INTEGER /* Total number of visits to this URL */,
  "is_internal" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the URL is internal to the site */,
  "page_title" TEXT /* Title of the web page */,
  "meta_description" TEXT /* Meta description of the web page */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("url_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transition_types" (
  "transition_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transition type */,
  "transition_type" TEXT /* Type of transition (e.g., typed URL, link click) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transition type */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if this is the default transition type */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the transition type was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transition_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment (e.g., marketing, user testing) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the segment was created */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the segment is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visit_sources" (
  "from_visit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the source visit */,
  "visit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the visit that originated from this source */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the source (e.g., homepage, search result) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the source (e.g., internal, external) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the source was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("from_visit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visit_opener" (
  "opener_visit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the opener visit */,
  "visit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the visit that was opened */,
  "opener_description" TEXT /* Description of the opener (e.g., new tab, popup) */,
  "opener_type" TEXT /* Type of the opener (e.g., tab, popup) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the opener was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("opener_visit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the session (in microseconds since epoch) */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the session (in microseconds since epoch) */,
  "total_duration" INTEGER /* Total duration of the session in microseconds */,
  "number_of_visits" INTEGER /* Number of visits in the session */,
  "session_type" TEXT /* Type of the session (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the session is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the session was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "session_visits" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session */,
  "visit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the visit within the session */,
  "visit_order" INTEGER /* Order of the visit within the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id", "visit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., analyst, data scientist, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, delete) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the role was created */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the role is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
